needle_load.note: Claude logs ~5s idle before needle_load; combined idle+needle_load Claude=12s vs GPT-4o=8s (delta 4s).

suture_placement.note: GPT-4o extends placement window further into the first pull-through.

first_throw.note: Claude absorbs more of the tail-organization time into first_throw.

third_throw.note: GPT-4o attributes the extra security throws to third_throw, Claude closes third earlier.

suture_cut.note: Unusually tight cut-phase agreement for a V-series video. Sum of third_throw+cut: Claude 73s vs GPT-4o 86.8s (delta 13.8s) — wider than V11/V12/V14/V16.

extra_security_throws.note: Both teachers flag additional throws beyond the required three as a major efficiency loss.

gap_visible.note: Drain-gap divergence recurs. Same pattern as V11/V14/V16 — monitor-recording bias. Claude flags partial closure with visible gap (low confidence), GPT-4o sees near-complete closure.

placement_alignment.note: Exact agreement on placement deviation, both with low confidence due to phone-recording-of-monitor format.

fls_score_reconciliation.reconciliation_note: Strong agreement — ΔFLS only 1.2 points, one of the tightest convergences in the V-series. Near-identical placement calls and near-identical total time, with the penalty delta (4.0) almost entirely explained by the recurring Claude drain-gap flag. Midpoint 435.6 is a reasonable reconciled value; GPT-4o's 436.2 is likely closer to a human proctor given its higher confidence (0.58 vs 0.42) and clean drain call.

systematic_findings.drain_gap_bias_pattern_continues: V17 extends the monitor-resolution drain-gap bias pattern. Confirmed cases: V11 (gap), V14 (gap), V16 (gap), V17 (gap). Counter-example remains V12 (agreement). The pattern is strongly associated with phone-recording-of-monitor format regardless of nominal resolution. The gap_visible signal from Claude should be down-weighted for training when the recording format is monitor-capture.
systematic_findings.phase_boundary_pattern_variant: V17 shows a variant of the third_throw/suture_cut boundary issue. In V11/V12/V14/V16 the two phases mirror (sum converges within ~4s). In V17 the sum diverges by ~14s because the disagreement extends further back into first_throw and second_throw — Claude places ~37s of tying time in throws 1+2, GPT-4o only ~22s. Hypothesis: V17's extra security throws blur the throw-count boundary for Claude, pulling late work into earlier phase labels. Training signal guidance still stands — use total_time + penalties, not per-phase durations.
systematic_findings.endpoint_ambiguity: Both teachers flag that V17 ends while the cut phase is still active — instruments are still near the knot at the final frame. Neither scorer can confirm both tails are fully divided. Both used end-of-clip as a conservative completion proxy. This is the first V-series video where both teachers explicitly note endpoint uncertainty.
